Notice Of Motion To Compel Defendant Embroker Insurance Services Llc To Provide Further Responses To Demand For Inspection And To Produce Documents; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-01-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. MARK E. HELLENKAMP, Esq. of Clark Hill LLP (Tel: 619-557-0404) for plaintiffs;
Atty. TERI MAE RUTLEDGE, Esq. of Cozen O'Connor for defendants Clear Blue Specialty Insurance Company and Embroker Insurance Services LLC.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS APOLLO BROKERS, INC., JOHN LOEBER, ALEX BECKER, and JOHN MCNAMARA's Motion To Compel Defendant Embroker Insurance Services LLC To Provide Further Responses To Demand For Inspection And To Produce Documents; Request For Sanctions.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with modifications, as follows:

Further responses shall be timely produced with regard to Requests 6, 14, 15, 18, 19, 22, 25, 27, 31, 34-35, 39, 41, and 42. Requests 26, 28, and 36 are waived by plaintiff's counsel at the hearing. All other requests for further responses are denied. Requests 2 & 7 are denied without prejudice. The requests for further production that are granted may produce relevant responses. It will be up to the trial court to determine relevance. The objections as to overbreadth and burden are not supported by any admissible declaration from Defendant detailing the alleged burden of production. Declarations are necessary to show how these burdens manifest, in labor, in time, and in dollar expense. Yes discovery is burdensome. Yes, it consumes time and treasure. The victor in the balancing test is the one who offers the court facts as to how this particular discovery demand is not just burdensome but unduly burdensome-i.e. excessively and inappropriately challenging, given the expected benefits.

Plaintiffs' counsel to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at tom@thomascohen.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
Off Calendar Jan-12-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT EMBROKER INSURANCE SERVICES LLC's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.

Defendant Embroker Insurance Services LLC's motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied in part and granted in part. The motion has two parts:

Intentional Misrepresentation. Insurer's agents - e.g., Embroker - may be liable for the agents' misrepresentations. (Bock v. Hansen (2014) 225 Cal.App.4th 215, 228-32.) The opinions Embroker cites - and Bock distinguishes - are not persuasive. The motion's first part is thus denied.

Tortious Interference With Contract. Plaintiffs effectively concede the motion's second part by saying they can amend this cause of action to make it cognizable. This part is granted with leave to amend.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
